etymology.description: 佛(佛) (fó, "Buddha"), 教(教) (jiào, "teach; religion"), 徒(徒) (tú, "disciple; follower")
etymology.interpretation: 佛(佛) (fó, "Buddha") refers to the enlightened being central to this religion. 教(教) (jiào, "teach; religion") denotes a systematic teaching or organized belief system. Thus, 佛教(佛教) (fójiào, "Buddhism") means the religious teachings of Buddha. 徒(徒) (tú, "disciple; follower") refers to someone who adheres to or practices these teachings. Together, 佛教徒(佛教徒) (fójiàotú, "Buddhist") describes a person who follows the teachings of Buddhism or is a disciple of Buddha.

examples.chinese: 他是一位佛教徒(他是一位佛教徒)。
很多佛教徒(很多佛教徒)会去寺庙祷告(會去寺廟禱告)。
examples.pinyin: Hěn duō Fójiàotú huì qù sìmiào dǎogào.
examples.english: Many Buddhists go to the temple to pray.

examples.chinese: 佛教徒们(佛教徒們)相信因果法则(相信因果法則)。
examples.english: Buddhists believe in the law of karma.
